title: Runtime Sanitizers, Dynamic Analysis, and Language Profiles · Repository Factory
summary: Add runtime instrumentation to native and managed-language test profiles. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of a sanitizer and dynamic-analysis matrix while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Use AddressSanitizer builds to detect memory safety defects in native code.
Use UndefinedBehaviorSanitizer with appropriate recover or trap policy.
Select thread, memory, leak, race, or platform sanitizers according to risk and compatibility.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# Risk-profiled repository gate portfolio
# Each gate needs purpose, command, failure fixture, owner, and suppression policy.

steps.title: Commit to the mission model
steps.instruction:
Use a changed workshop case derived from this module: gates sanitizers. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

Use a changed workshop case derived from this module: gates sanitizers. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result. What must be established before implementation?

content.text: Use AddressSanitizer builds to detect memory safety defects in native code. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Check preconditions, perform the operation in an inspectable order, then verify the result and a boundary case.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Use a changed worked example case derived from this module: gates sanitizers. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result. A defensible solution applies Asan, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to a sanitizer and dynamic-analysis matrix.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: Passing ordinary tests proves memory access is safe. This fails the Asan contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

steps.title: Extend the cumulative artifact
steps.instruction: Append a gates-sanitizer-ci-dyqv39: manifest entry with concrete verify plus failure, owner, or repair evidence.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Sanitizer Ci into observable performance evidence.

content.code:
gates-sanitizer-ci-dyqv39:
  verify: [replace with a deterministic check]
  failure: [replace with the rejected condition]
  repair: [replace with a local reproduction and correction path]

options.text: Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.
Reject the unsupported conclusion, name the violated Sanitizer Ci condition, repair the responsible layer, and rerun the boundary trace.
Keep the plausible output and hide the assumption to avoid confusing the stakeholder.

steps.title: Defend the transferred model
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Sanitizer Ci applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Run expensive dynamic checks in a documented matrix with symbolized actionable output.
